name: Email Address
description: Validates a standard email address format. Checks for a local part, an @ symbol, a domain, and a top-level domain of at least two characters.
notes: Does not perform DNS lookup or mailbox verification. Very long TLDs and internationalized domain names (IDN) may require a more permissive pattern.

name: URL (HTTP/HTTPS)
description: Validates HTTP and HTTPS URLs including optional www prefix, path, query string, and fragment. Does not validate other protocols such as ftp or ws.
notes: Does not validate internationalized URLs (IRI). For production use, prefer a URL parsing library over regex.

name: IPv4 Address
description: Validates a dotted-decimal IPv4 address. Each octet must be a number between 0 and 255.
notes: Does not validate CIDR notation or private vs public ranges. Leading zeros (e.g. 01.02.03.04) are rejected.

name: IPv6 Address
description: Validates a full or compressed IPv6 address, including link-local addresses with zone IDs and IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses.
notes: Does not validate zone IDs containing arbitrary characters. For embedded use in URLs, IPv6 addresses require square bracket notation.

name: Phone Number (E.164)
description: Validates an international phone number in E.164 format. Must start with a '+' followed by a country code and subscriber number, with a total length of 7 to 15 digits.
notes: Does not validate whether the country code or number is actually assigned. Does not accept spaces, dashes, or parentheses.

name: Date (ISO 8601)
description: Validates a calendar date in ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DD format. Month must be 01–12 and day must be 01–31.
notes: Does not validate that the day is valid for the given month (e.g. 2023-02-30 would pass). Use date parsing to catch invalid calendar combinations.

name: Datetime (ISO 8601)
description: Validates an ISO 8601 datetime string with mandatory time zone offset or UTC designator (Z). Supports optional fractional seconds.
notes: Does not validate calendar correctness of the date portion. Timezone offset range is limited to UTC-23:59 to UTC+23:59.

name: Hex Color Code
description: Validates a CSS hex color code with the leading '#'. Accepts 3-digit (shorthand RGB), 4-digit (shorthand RGBA), 6-digit (full RGB), and 8-digit (full RGBA) forms.
notes: The 4- and 8-digit alpha forms are supported in CSS Colors Level 4 but may not work in older browsers. Case-insensitive.

name: UUID (v1–v5)
description: Validates a UUID in canonical 8-4-4-4-12 hyphenated format. Accepts versions 1 through 5 and enforces the variant bits in the 17th character.
notes: Does not validate UUIDs v6, v7, or v8 (newer formats with different version nibble values). The nil UUID (all zeros) will fail due to version byte constraint.

name: URL Slug
description: Validates a URL-friendly slug consisting of lowercase letters and digits, optionally separated by single hyphens. No leading or trailing hyphens, and no consecutive hyphens.
notes: Does not allow uppercase letters or underscores. For slugs that permit underscores, replace the hyphen character class with [-_].

name: Username
description: Validates a username between 2 and 30 characters. Must start and end with an alphanumeric character; may contain letters, digits, underscores, hyphens, and dots in between.
notes: Length and character rules vary across platforms. Adjust the quantifier {1,28} to change the allowed range. Does not prevent reserved words.

name: Strong Password
description: Validates a password of at least 12 characters that contains at least one lowercase letter, one uppercase letter, one digit, and one special character.
notes: Lookahead-based rules encourage diversity but do not measure entropy. NIST SP 800-63B recommends prioritizing length and checking against breached password lists over complexity rules.

name: Credit Card Number (Luhn)
description: Validates the format of common credit card numbers including Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, and Diners Club. Does not perform the Luhn checksum in the regex itself.
notes: Regex validates card network format only. Always run the Luhn algorithm in code to verify the check digit. Never log or store raw card numbers; use a PCI-DSS compliant tokenization service.

name: German Postal Code
description: Validates a German postcode (Postleitzahl), which consists of exactly 5 digits.
notes: Does not validate whether the code is actually assigned by Deutsche Post. Some valid-format codes may not correspond to a real location.

name: US ZIP Code
description: Validates a US ZIP Code in 5-digit format or ZIP+4 format (e.g. 12345-6789). The 4-digit extension is optional.
notes: Does not validate whether the ZIP code is actually assigned by USPS. The range 00001–99999 includes many unassigned codes.

name: Semantic Version
description: Validates a semantic version string following the semver.org specification. Supports optional pre-release identifiers (e.g. -alpha.1) and build metadata (e.g. +build.20240101).
notes: Leading 'v' prefix (common in Git tags) is not part of the semver spec and will fail validation. Strip it before matching if needed.

name: JSON Web Token (JWT)
description: Validates the structural format of a JSON Web Token: three Base64URL-encoded segments separated by dots. Does not verify the signature or decode the payload.
notes: A structurally valid token may still be expired, tampered with, or use an unsupported algorithm. Always verify the signature server-side using a trusted JWT library.

name: Markdown Heading
description: Matches a Markdown ATX-style heading. Captures the hash characters (group 1, indicating level 1–6) and the heading text (group 2).
notes: Use the multiline flag ('m') so ^ and $ match line boundaries within a larger document. Does not match Setext-style headings (underline with === or ---).

name: HTML Tag
description: Matches a single HTML tag, either self-closing or with an inline closing tag. Captures the tag name in group 1.
notes: Regex is fundamentally unsuited for parsing nested or complex HTML. Use a proper HTML parser (DOMDocument in PHP, DOMParser in JS) for any real document processing. This pattern is only appropriate for simple, controlled matching.

name: Base64 String
description: Validates a standard Base64-encoded string. Enforces correct padding with '=' characters at the end.
notes: Does not validate Base64URL (which uses '-' and '_' instead of '+' and '/'). An empty string matches because all groups are optional. For URL-safe Base64 replace +/ with \-_.
